mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflect capacity constraints and slowing output, which compress corporate profit margins and generate negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases, invalidating the profit margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms underlying economic expansion, which provides a persistent positive baseline for S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5% annually forces central banks to prioritize inflation control over growth, raising real discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above the 2.5% threshold are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical instances, contradicting the discount rate mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a hit rate above 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions during recessionary periods reflect severe demand destruction and supply chain breakdowns, triggering immediate negative equity repricing within one trading week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessionary phases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 65% of cases, disproving the immediate repricing mechanism.
